title: pelargonium alternans subsp. parviinflorescens
intro: Pelargonium alternans subsp. parviinflorescens is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Geraniaceae, commonly known as the "small-flowered" or " dwarf" Pelargonium. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, primarily due to the presence of compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The plant has been reported to possess antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which may contribute to its potential health benefits, including immune system support and wound healing. Herbal preparations of Pelargonium alternans subsp. parviinflorescens may be used in various forms, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
